responseMessageContents.text:
## Goal
- Fix the QR code flow that was opening `api.qrserver.com` image URLs in Chrome (causing crashes) by rendering QR codes in-app via local generation, and deploy the fix to production.

## Constraints & Preferences
- React Native (Expo), NOT Swift — user explicitly rejected iOS native
- No hardcoded arrays, static JSON, mock files, or local device storage for tasks
- PRODUCTION_VERIFIED display requires five-point gate: real commit SHA, real deployment ID, health HTTP 200, running commit match, QA evidence
- Forbidden evidence values: AUTO-GENERATED, UNKNOWN, PENDING, PLACEHOLDER, MOCK, NARRATIVE, GENERATED, SIMULATED, ESTIMATED, ASSUMED
- Destructive tasks (mass data deletion) require explicit owner approval — never auto-executed
- GitHub repo `ibb142/rork-global-real-estate-invest` is PUBLIC, pushed via Rork-managed GitHub App binding (all `ghp_` tokens revoked)
- Backend: `https://ivx-holdings-platform.onrender.com` (Render), Supabase: `https://kvclcdjmjghndxsngfzb.supabase.co`
- QR image URLs must NEVER be opened in browser — must be rendered as images inside the app
- QR codes should be generated locally from `QR_DESTINATION_URL` using the project's own QR component, not fetched from public endpoints
- Must separate: `QR_IMAGE_URL` (image displayed in-app), `QR_DESTINATION_URL` (link encoded in QR), `RETURN_URL` (route to return to IVX)
- No secrets/tokens/passwords in QR payloads
- Required QR viewer UI: modal/screen, centered QR, title, explanation, close/back/share/copy buttons, loading state, error state, retry button
- Android hardware back must return to previous IVX screen; no auto browser open; no white screen/crash on invalid data

## Progress
### Done
- **Canonical media migration fully deployed and verified** — commit `1103b9188401029bcd5ae3b37da5e34f469ebaed` pushed to main, Render deployed, migration applied (33/33 statements), 24 media rows, 1 reel, 0 orphans, 0 duplicates, all three projects serving canonical source
- **Task dispositions live** — 60 total, 45 PRODUCTION_VERIFIED, 1 BLOCKED_OWNER_APPROVAL (`task-d8e11135-0502-4bdf-af1f-c4eca5227ef0`), 14 CANCELLED_OBSOLETE, 0 plain BLOCKED — verified via live API
- **Commit chain**: `69731cb3` → `d975b782` → `635d897d` → `ae137df4` → `1103b918` (current live)
- **QR audit complete** — found `api.qrserver.com` in `expo/constants/platform-config.ts:357` (`generateTrackableLink`); three Image-based QR render sites: `app/admin/marketing.tsx` (lines 1879, 2533), `app/admin/influencers.tsx` (line 918); crash path identified: `openAttachment()` in `ivxChat.ts` + `handleOpenAttachment` in `app/ivx/chat.tsx` both call `Linking.openURL` on any URL including QR image URLs
- **QR URL utilities created**: `expo/lib/qr-url.ts` — `isQrImageUrl()`, `extractQrDestinationUrl()`, `validateQrImageUrl()` (HTTP 200 + Content-Type image/* + timeout), `logQrDiagnostics()` (payload-free), `newQrTraceId()`, `safeUrlHost()`
- **In-app QR viewer modal created**: `expo/components/QRViewerModal.tsx` — renders QR locally via `QRCodeView` from destination URL, falls back to validated remote image only if no destination, loading/error/retry states, share/copy/back/close buttons, `onRequestClose` for Android hardware back
- **Chat crash path patched** (`expo/app/ivx/chat.tsx`) — `handleOpenAttachment` now intercepts `isQrImageUrl()` and opens `QRViewerModal` instead of `Linking.openURL`; modal rendered before `</ErrorBoundary>`
- **Chat service guard added** (`expo/src/modules/chat/services/ivxChat.ts`) — `openAttachment()` throws if URL is a QR image URL (defense in depth)
- **MessageBubble patched** (`expo/src/modules/chat/components/MessageBubble.tsx`) — intercepts QR image URLs in `handleOpenAttachment`, opens `QRViewerModal` inline
- **Admin marketing QR switched** (`expo/app/admin/marketing.tsx`) — both Image sources replaced with `QRCodeView` local generation from `contentLink.fullUrl` / `selectedLink.fullUrl`
- **Admin influencers QR switched** (`expo/app/admin/influencers.tsx`) — Image source replaced with `QRCodeView` from referral URL, "Download QR" button changed to "Share QR" using `Share.share()`
- **Existing local QR components confirmed working**: `expo/components/QRCodeView.tsx` (SVG-based, uses `expo/lib/qr-generator.ts`), already used correctly in `expo/app/qr-code.tsx` and `expo/app/business-card.tsx`

### In Progress
- **Need to typecheck, test, commit, push, deploy, and verify production** — all QR fix code changes are written but not yet committed or deployed; no typecheck or test run yet
- **Need to verify on Android, iOS, and web** — open/close/reopen/rotate/back/offline/invalid-data/restart scenarios per the urgent fix requirements

### Blocked
- **15th blocked task still requires owner approval** — "Delete all user data from the production database" (`task-d8e11135-0502-4bdf-af1f-c4eca5227ef0`) is destructive, irreversible, no backup; held as `BLOCKED_OWNER_APPROVAL` pending explicit owner command

## Key Decisions
- QR codes are generated locally from the destination URL using the existing `QRCodeView` SVG component — no dependency on `api.qrserver.com` for rendering
- `api.qrserver.com` URLs are detected by `isQrImageUrl()` (host + path + `data` param patterns) and intercepted before any `Linking.openURL` call
- Remote QR image fallback (if ever needed) is validated with HTTP 200 + `image/*` Content-Type + timeout before being rendered as an `<Image>` — never navigated to
- `generateTrackableLink()` in `platform-config.ts` still generates `qrCodeUrl` pointing to `api.qrserver.com` but all consumers now use `fullUrl` with local `QRCodeView` instead — the `qrCodeUrl` field is effectively unused for display
- Diagnostics log route, component, action, image request status, destination validity, navigation target, platform, trace ID — never the QR payload itself

## Next Steps
1. Run typecheck (`tsc --noEmit`) on all modified files to catch any compile errors
2. Run existing tests to ensure no regressions
3. Commit all QR fix changes, push to GitHub main
4. Wait for Render autodeploy, verify health endpoint shows new commit
5. Verify no `api.qrserver.com` URL appears in any `Linking.openURL` or `window.open` call path
6. Verify QR viewer modal renders locally, no Chrome tab opens
7. Run Android/iOS/web QA: open/close/reopen/rotate/back/offline/invalid-data/restart
8. Return evidence block with `QR_SOURCE_FILE`, `ROOT_CAUSE`, `COMMIT_SHA`, `DEPLOYMENT_OR_UPDATE_ID`, all QA pass/fail values

## Critical Context
- **GitHub HEAD**: `1103b9188401029bcd5ae3b37da5e34f469ebaed` on `main` (migration commit)
- **Render running commit**: `1103b9188401029bcd5ae3b37da5e34f469ebaed` (bootTime `2026-07-11T05:02:22.667Z`)
- **Workspace has uncommitted QR fix changes** — new files: `expo/lib/qr-url.ts`, `expo/components/QRViewerModal.tsx`; modified: `expo/app/ivx/chat.tsx`, `expo/src/modules/chat/services/ivxChat.ts`, `expo/src/modules/chat/components/MessageBubble.tsx`, `expo/app/admin/marketing.tsx`, `expo/app/admin/influencers.tsx`
- **Root cause**: `handleOpenAttachment` in `app/ivx/chat.tsx` and `openAttachment()` in `ivxChat.ts` called `Linking.openURL(message.attachmentUrl)` on any URL, including `api.qrserver.com` QR image URLs — Android opened Chrome to view the raw PNG
- **QR source**: `expo/constants/platform-config.ts:357` — `generateTrackableLink()` builds `qrCodeUrl: https://api.qrserver.com/v1/create-qr-code/?size=300x300&data=...`; admin screens displayed this via `<Image source={{uri: qrCodeUrl}}>` — now switched to local `QRCodeView`
- **`QRCodeView`** at `expo/components/QRCodeView.tsx` uses `react-native-svg` + `expo/lib/qr-generator.ts` (pure JS QR matrix generator, EC level M, versions 1-10)
- **Supabase `jv_deal_media`**: 24 rows (8 per project, 1 cover each), `jv_deal_reels`: 1 row (Casa Rosario tour), `ivx_project_integrity` view live
- **Owner-only routes return HTTP 401** without credentials — auth guard working correctly

## Relevant Files
- `expo/lib/qr-url.ts` — NEW: QR URL detection, destination extraction, image validation, diagnostics helpers
- `expo/components/QRViewerModal.tsx` — NEW: in-app QR viewer modal with local rendering, loading/error/retry, share/copy/back/close
- `expo/components/QRCodeView.tsx` — existing local SVG QR renderer (uses `expo/lib/qr-generator.ts`)
- `expo/lib/qr-generator.ts` — existing pure-JS QR matrix generator
- `expo/app/ivx/chat.tsx` — MODIFIED: `handleOpenAttachment` intercepts QR image URLs, renders `QRViewerModal`
- `expo/src/modules/chat/services/ivxChat.ts` — MODIFIED: `openAttachment()` throws on QR image URLs (defense in depth)
- `expo/src/modules/chat/components/MessageBubble.tsx` — MODIFIED: intercepts QR image URLs, renders `QRViewerModal` inline
- `expo/app/admin/marketing.tsx` — MODIFIED: two `<Image source={{uri: qrCodeUrl}}>` replaced with `<QRCodeView value={fullUrl}>`
- `expo/app/admin/influencers.tsx` — MODIFIED: `<Image>` replaced with `<QRCodeView>`, Download QR → Share QR
- `expo/constants/platform-config.ts` — `generateTrackableLink()` still generates `qrCodeUrl` field (now unused for display)
- `backend/services/ivx-canonical-media-migration.ts` — migration runner (deployed and applied)
- `backend/api/ivx-media-migration.ts` — GET status + POST apply endpoints (deployed)
- `backend/services/ivx-canonical-task-store.ts` — canonical task store with `BLOCKED_OWNER_APPROVAL` + `CANCELLED_OBSOLETE` (deployed)
- `backend/api/ivx-project-engagement.ts` — `handleProjectMediaGet` reads canonical tables first (deployed)
